content.problemStatement:
**This problem has the same setup and definitions as PARTMIN. However, they differ in what needs to be computed, as well as the constraint on NNN.**

Consider a tree on NNN vertices, with vertex uuu having value AuA_uAu​ written on it.

We have the following definitions:


For two vertices u,vu, vu,v, define P(u,v)P(u, v)P(u,v) to be the set of vertices on the unique simple path from uuu to vvv in the tree.
Define mn(u,v)\text{mn}(u, v)mn(u,v) to be the minimum value of AwA_wAw​ across all w∈P(u,v)w \in P(u, v)w∈P(u,v), that is, mn(u,v)\text{mn}(u, v)mn(u,v) is the minimum value on the path between uuu and vvv.
S={mn(u,v)∣1≤u<v≤N}S = \{ \text{mn}(u, v) \mid 1 \leq u \lt v \leq N \}S={mn(u,v)∣1≤u<v≤N}, i.e. SSS is the set of all path minimums of the tree, excluding paths consisting of a single vertex.
We then define score(A)\text{score}(A)score(A) to be the smallest **positive** integer that doesn't appear in SSS.

In simpler words, score(A)\text{score}(A)score(A) is the smallest positive integer that doesn't appear as the minimum of any path in the tree, when only considering paths with length at least 222.

You are given a tree on NNN vertices.

Compute the sum of score(A)\text{score}(A)score(A) over all possible arrays AAA that consist of distinct integers in [1,N][1, N][1,N], i.e., AAA should be a permutation of {1,2,…,N}\{1, 2, \ldots, N\}{1,2,…,N}.

As the answer may be large, output it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN — the number of vertices.
The next N−1N-1N−1 lines describe the edges of the tree. The iii-th of them contains two space-separated integers uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​, denoting an edge between these two vertices.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the sum of score(A)\text{score}(A)score(A) over all possible permutations AAA, modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The input edges describe a tree on NNN vertices.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 404040.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The possible arrays are A=[1,2]A = [1, 2]A=[1,2] and A=[2,1]A = [2, 1]A=[2,1]. They both have a score of 222, so the answer is 2+2=42+2 = 42+2=4.
Test case 222: There are 666 possible arrays. Of them, [2,1,3][2, 1, 3][2,1,3] and [3,1,2][3, 1, 2][3,1,2] have scores of 222, while the rest have scores of 333. So, the answer is 2⋅2+4⋅3=162\cdot 2 + 4\cdot 3 = 162⋅2+4⋅3=16.
